# TheSwitch

> Zero-config, zero-dependency, privacy-first adaptive theming. Include it and a
> site's theme reacts to time, season, sky, weather and (opt-in) location:
> light, dark, sunny, snow, windy, watery, foggy, stormy, night. Theming only —
> translation is the sibling library, Mr.Latin.

## What it does
Detects the visitor's "atmosphere" and applies a coherent set of CSS custom
properties (`--ts-*`) plus a `data-atmos-skin` attribute, so the page restyles
itself. With no opt-in it uses only the local clock + hemisphere (no network).

## Install / use
- npm: `npm install theswitch` then `import { TheSwitch } from "theswitch"; new TheSwitch().start();`
- CDN (no build): `<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/theswitch/dist/the-switch.global.js" data-the-switch></script>`

## Privacy tiers
- Default: time + season only — fully offline, zero network calls.
- Opt-in: `data-use-geolocation` (or `setLiveWeather(true)`) enables live weather via Open-Meteo (free, no key), coarse coordinates only.

## Core API
- `new TheSwitch(options).start(): Promise<TheSwitch>`
- methods: `setMode("auto"|"light"|"dark")`, `setLiveWeather(on)`, `subscribe(fn) => unsubscribe`, `getState()`, `destroy()`
- options: `mode`, `latitude`, `longitude`, `useGeolocation`, `refreshMinutes`, `widget`, `presets`, `transition`
- exports: `detectAtmosphere`, `deriveSkin`, `applyTheme`, `clearTheme`, `SKIN_PRESETS`, `fetchWeather`
- skins: light, dark, sunny, snow, windy, watery, foggy, stormy, night

## Styling for AI agents / consumers
- Style the page with the `--ts-*` custom properties and/or `[data-atmos-skin="snow"]`-style selectors. Built-in presets exist for every skin; override per-skin via `applyTheme(skin, el, { presets })` or the `presets` option.
- `data-atmos-*` attributes (skin / theme / daypart / season / weather) are set on the root for CSS targeting.

## Links
- README.md — full documentation and examples
- CONTRIBUTING.md — zero-dependency, theming-only, privacy-first rules
- SECURITY.md — privacy and network policy
- Sibling: Mr.Latin — drop-in page translation
